body.title: [hold] Google Secretary rack device — calendar/email/tasks via bus
body.result: Akien: privacy/identity complexities on inspectable bus not yet resolved; waiting on design for requestor identity model
body.description:
Build GoogleSecretaryDevice as a first-class rack device under devices/google_secretary/. Other rack devices (Igor, Granny) send structured requests via the bus; the Secretary handles all Google Workspace operations and replies. Replaces the stale T-uc-gmail-google utility-closet approach.

Core design: graph-tree dispatcher routes inbound requests to the right Google operation (calendar CRUD, email send/read/forward/search, tasks) and escalates to the human channel when intent is ambiguous. Auth is OAuth-based with token storage in Postgres or filesystem (no SQLite). BPA script suite via SWADL handles routine flows.

**Affected files:** devices/google_secretary/ (new — device.py, shim.py, dispatcher.py, tools/calendar.py, tools/gmail.py, tools/tasks.py, bpa/); config/profiles/google_secretary.yaml (new); skeleton/registry.py (register device); tests/google_secretary/ (new)

**Design rules:** no-sqlite (tokens in Postgres or flat-file); bus+shim model (device inherits BaseDevice, exposes MCP tools via skeleton); devices/ isolation (independently deployable, no cross-device imports); log every interface crossing (AR-009)

**Scope boundary:** IN — device skeleton + lifecycle (start/stop/restart/self_test); graph-tree dispatcher with escalate-to-channel; calendar CRUD; gmail send/read/forward/search; tasks CRUD; OAuth token management; BPA/SWADL script suite for routine ops; MCP tool registration so Igor/Granny can call via bus. OUT — login/2FA flows (T-google-secretary-login); production credentials setup (human step); Drive/Contacts (deferred follow-on)

**Completion criteria:** (1) devices/google_secretary/device.py passes self_test() with mocked Google APIs; (2) calendar create+read+delete tools callable via MCP; (3) gmail send+read tools callable via MCP; (4) dispatcher routes a structured request to the right tool and returns result; (5) ambiguous request triggers escalate-to-channel, not a guess; (6) all tests in tests/google_secretary/ green; (7) skeleton/registry.py lists google_secretary; (8) pip install -e . succeeds
